title: Cattan, Rodrigues & Congedo 2019 — Passive Head-Mounted Display Music-Listening EEG dataset (PHMD)
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalographic recordings from 12 healthy subjects during music listening with and without a passive head-mounted display (PHMD). The study employed a resting-state paradigm with 16-channel EEG acquisition at 512 Hz, comparing neural activity across alternating blocks of smartphone-on and smartphone-off conditions. The dataset provides raw, unfiltered EEG data in mat and csv formats, facilitating investigation of how passive head-mounted displays influence cortical activity during auditory stimulation.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired from 12 subjects using a 16-channel g.USBamp system (sampling rate: 512 Hz) with wet electrodes in a standard 10-20 montage (reference: right earlobe, ground: AFz). Subjects sat approximately 50 cm from a screen and listened to Bach Inventions while maintaining visual fixation on a marker. Each experimental session consisted of 10 blocks (60 seconds each) with randomized alternation between smartphone switched-off and switched-on conditions. Data were recorded without digital filtering or Faraday cage shielding to simulate real-world usage conditions.
